title: Server-Survival: Tower Catch
description: Drag a rescue cradle around a collapsing server tower to catch clean traffic modules, dodge bot junk, and keep the stack online through surges.

howToPlayJson.title: Server-Survival: Tower Catch
howToPlayJson.objective: Survive a 3-minute traffic siege by catching the right falling infrastructure pieces to stabilize the tower while avoiding harmful packets, overload debris, and bad scaling choices.

controls.input: Drag left or right with one thumb along the bottom rail
controls.outcome: Moves the load-balancer cradle under falling items

controls.input: Tap with a second thumb while dragging
controls.outcome: Flips the cradle between App mode and Cache mode for 3 seconds, changing what counts as safe to catch

controls.input: Double-tap the cradle when the meter is full
controls.outcome: Triggers a short autoscale pulse that clears one wave of junk and slows drops

steps: Clean request blocks, cache capsules, and server blades fall from the tower lanes above.
Catch items that match the cradle's current mode to patch the tower and build combo.
Avoid bot packets, corrupted logs, and overheated hardware chunks; each bad catch costs tower health.
Every few seconds a traffic spike changes demand: App mode may become dangerous while Cache mode scores double, or the reverse.
Survive escalating waves as drop speed, fake decoys, and mixed traffic patterns increase until final meltdown timer ends.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Score 10 points per correct catch, plus a combo multiplier that rises every 3 clean catches up to x5. Catching the demanded traffic type during a spike gives bonus points and extends combo time. Any bad catch, miss on a
tips: Stay near center so one-thumb slides can reach either lane before a spike lands.
Use reroute only when the spike banner changes; flipping too early turns safe pieces into hazards.
Build combo on common traffic, then spend autoscale burst when bot swarms crowd the screen.
Watch tower windows: flashing blue means cache demand, flashing green means app demand.
